flashcards.back: A collection of interconnected virtual machines (VMs) deployed and managed as a unified resource pool on top of physical host infrastructure.

flashcards.front: What is the key technology that enables Live Migration?
flashcards.back: The iterative pre-copy of memory pages, followed by a brief stop-and-copy of the remaining state.

flashcards.front: Name one primary benefit of server consolidation.
flashcards.back: Reduced energy consumption by powering down underutilized physical servers.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between Horizontal and Vertical Scaling?
flashcards.back: Horizontal scaling adds/removes VMs (scale-out/in). Vertical scaling adds/removes resources (e.g., CPU, RAM) to an existing VM (scale-up/down).

flashcards.front: What is the role of a Cluster Manager?
flashcards.back: To provision, manage, monitor, and automate resource allocation for a collection of VMs and hosts.
